definitions: In a languid manner, without force or effort, in a manner requiring little energy or exertion.
examples: He languidly waved his limp wrist, glad to see us, but not wanting to expend the energy to let us know that.

definitions: To lose strength and become weak; to be in a state of weakness or sickness. [from 14th c.]
To be neglected; to make little progress, be unsuccessful. [from 17th c.]
To pine away in longing for something; to have low spirits, especially from lovesickness. [from 14th c.]
To live in miserable or disheartening conditions. [from 15th c.]

definitions: Someone who languishes.

A state of the body or mind caused by exhaustion or disease and characterized by a languid or weary feeling; lassitude; (countable) an instance of this.
Listless indolence or inactivity, especially if enjoyable or relaxing; dreaminess; (countable) an instance of this.
Dullness, sluggishness; lack of vigour; stagnation.
Heavy humidity and stillness of the air.
examples: The evening was mild, with a certain languor in the air.

definitions: To languish.
lacking energy, spirit, liveliness or vitality; languid, lackadaisical.
examples: Warm as a Lesbian valley's afternoon Made langourous [sic] with June

definitions: In a languorous manner; without exertion; lazily.
examples: I stretched languorously, and then remained in bed.

definitions: Any of the Old World monkeys of the genera Simias, Trachypithecus (lutungs), Presbytis (surilis), and Semnopithecus (gray langurs).

definitions: Slender or thin; not well filled out; not plump; shrunken; lean.

definitions: The state or condition of being lanky.
examples: In his civil dress his lankiness and length of limb gave him an awkward appearance, but on horse-back, with his lean, gaunt face looking out from his steel cap, his breastplate and buff jacket filling out his figure, and his high boots of untanned leather reaching to the centre of his thighs, he looked the veteran man-at-arms.

examples: The batwings, full of tulips in May, are now abloom with cosmos, angelonia and lantana.

definitions: To furnish with a lantern.

definitions: The upper legs of a seated person.

Fore-and-aft rigs come in a variety of shapes, but the medieval Mediterranean lateen was triangular, and this too was an asset.

The shape of the lateen sail is triangular, or precisely that of a large fore-sail; it requires a long tapering yard to spread it, which will slightly bow or bend towards the middle when a stiff breeze fills the sail; a boom is used for some lateen sails, but not for all.

definitions: A dubious or disreputable person or thing

definitions: To make (an alcoholic beverage, e.g. absinthe or ouzo) cloudy by mixing it with water, due to the presence of anethole. This is known as the ouzo effect.

definitions: Not reputable or decent.

Washington -esque School For Gifted Youngsters vs. a rogue's gallery of evil mutants, crew cuts, and politicos—has gotten a lot murkier.

definitions: (colloquial) A loud sound or part of a sound.

[…] here and there a gray rock cropped out of the ground, and over it the yellow stone-crop and scarlet-leaved crane's-bill grew luxuriantly.

Hortense, with her rich chestnut locks so luxuriantly knotted, plaited, twisted, as if she did not know how to dispose of all their abundance, with her vermillion lips, damask cheek, and roguish laughing eye.

definitions: To be luxuriant; to grow exuberantly.
To enjoy luxury, to indulge.
examples: Luxuriate in the wonderful service of our five-star hotel.

definitions: The act of enjoying or experiencing luxury.
examples: Oliver. I’m sure he does. The wavy he suns himself here—like a cat purring in his luxuriation.

definitions: Very fine in quality and comfortable.
examples: Invalids, and luxurious persons, require more artificial arrangements for travelling on the camel.
a luxurious hotel suite

definitions: In a manner that involves luxury; in an expensive or indulgent manner.
The state or property of being luxurious, of being expensive or indulgently pleasant.
examples: The luxuriousness of the spa was present even in the expensive paper fittingly used for the bill.

definitions: Something desirable but expensive and that one can live without.
Something that is pleasant and desirable but not necessary in life (whether expensive or not).
Very wealthy and comfortable surroundings; the state of being that they create.
examples: Let not the Royall Bed of Denmark be / A Couch for Luxury and damned Incest.

definitions: Not essential but desirable and enjoyable and indulgent.

A member of the taxonomic family Lycaenidae, the gossamer-winged butterflies.

A werewolf.

(mythology, by extension) The state of being a person who can shapeshift between the form of a human being and an animal, whether or not it is a wolf.

(mythology) The state of being a lycanthrope (or werewolf), a person who can shapeshift between the form of a human being and a wolf, often said to happen involuntarily during a full moon; werewolfdom.
examples: On one hand, Jacques is quite glad that he doesn’t have classic lycanthropy. Turning into a squirrel tends to cause fewer problems than turning into a wolf.

definitions: An association for literary improvement.
examples: At a lyceum, not long since, I felt that the lecturer had chosen a theme too foreign to himself, and so failed to interest me as much as he might have done.
We burst out laughing. She told me that one of her teachers at the " lyceum " used to say that whenever any of the students got up to anything.

definitions: That tree's bright red oval fruit with a single stone surrounded by a fleshy white aril.

A soft pink- red colour, like that of a lychee rind (also called lychee red).
examples: No Chinese meal is complete without some succulent dim sims (pork minced with water chestnuts and enclosed in paste), and such sweets as honeyed lychee nuts and honeyed ginger.

definitions: (botany) Any of the genus Lychnis of flowering plants.

examples: And eating avocado with salsa boosted the absorption of lycopene —a carotenoid in tomatoes—by almost five times.

definitions: (botany) A club moss.
(botany) Any member of the Lycopodiophyta.
A club moss of the genus Lycopodium.
examples: We passed a torrent of water which is not much lower than the boiling point, and has a most singular appearance as it foams over its rugged bed, sending up clouds of steam, and often concealed by the overhanging herbage of ferns and lycopodia, which here thrive with more luxuriance than elsewhere.

definitions: plural of lydion
(UK, rail transport) A short side line, connected with the main line; a turn-out; a siding.
An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
Potassium or sodium hydroxide (caustic soda).

Brakevan lye. [same page in the main text] There is also an inclined lye for brakevans at each end of the yard.

definitions: To treat with lye.

present participle and gerund of lie (“ to intentionally give false information ”)

definitions: (physiology) A colourless, watery, coagulable bodily fluid which bathes the tissues and is carried by the lymphatic system into the bloodstream; it resembles blood plasma in containing white blood cells and especially lymphocytes but normally few red blood cells and no platelets.
examples: She lay face-down, an infected puncture point on the inside of her thigh oozing a faint lymph.

definitions: (biology, medicine) The inflammation or swelling of the lymph nodes, often a nonspecific sign of infection but also often benign and of obscure origin.

(medicine) dilation of the lymphatic vessels
A diagnostic image produced by lymphangiography.
A radiographic procedure for imaging the lymphatic system.

(pathology) Inflammation of the lymphatic channels, the result of infection at a site distal to the channel.

definitions: (anatomy) A vessel that transports lymph.
